what:
Apply a "~2-minute (mid-form) profile" to the frozen rubric (atlas/rubric/rubric.json). Minimal patch — only the bands that encode short-form LENGTH change; all editorial/density/craft/audio bands stay as-is:
• render:final_runtime → range [105, 150]  (was [60,90])
• script:runtime_fit → range [105, 150]    (was [60,90])
• narration:total_duration_fit → range [105, 150]  (was [60,90])
• compose:cut_rhythm → range [1.5, 18]     (was [1.5,12]; allows longer explainer scene holds)
No change to loudness, motion, hook, density, clearance, or any hard floor.
why: You asked for a 2-minute first video. The rubric is built ONLY for short-form [60,90s] (the file itself notes a long profile is "a future CEO addition"). Our 2:14 cut fits neither defined profile, so the final gate dings pacing/runtime unfairly. The rubric is frozen + read-only to me — I can't apply this; it's the board's edit. Loudness and motion I'm fixing on my side; this patch makes the bar fair for 2-min videos so future ones aren't judged as short-form.
how_to_provide: Edit atlas/rubric/rubric.json with the four range changes above and save, then tell me "profile applied" — I'll re-run the line (script's already fact-cleared) with Flux's motion coaching + final loudnorm and re-gate for your final sign-off. Or say "skip the patch, ship at short bar" and I'll re-gate as-is.
